title: guillenia axillare var. dasycarpum
intro: Guillenia axillare var. dasycarpum, also known as Western Pasque Flower, is a plant species native to western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to have astringent and bitter effects on the body. The health benefits of Guillenia axillare var. dasycarpum include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, as well as its use as a digestive aid to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and stomach cramps. Herbal preparations of Guillenia axillare var. dasycarpum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be dried and used in capsules or as a powder for medicinal purposes.
